title: Statistical Mechanics Deep II: Onsager, Transfer Matrices & Partition Functions

onsager_ising.note: Onsager: F=EML-3 (elliptic integral); critical exponents=EML-2; transition=EML-∞

eml_depth_summary.EML-1: Z = Σ exp(-βE) (partition function = sum of EML-1)
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: F = -log(Z)/β, entropy S = EML-2; critical exponents
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Onsager exact solution (elliptic integral); transfer matrix

key_theorem: The EML Statistical Mechanics Theorem (S204): Z = Σ exp(-βE): each term is EML-1, but the sum = EML-1 (closed under addition). Free energy F = -log(Z)/β = EML-2 (log of EML-1 = EML-2). Onsager exact 2D Ising = EML-3 (elliptic integral = EML-3). Phase transitions = EML-∞ (symmetry breaking, divergent correlation length). The Z → F → S ladder = EML-1 → EML-2 → EML-2 = standard partition function depth chain.
rabbit_hole_log: Z=EML-1: partition function is the archetype of the universal EML-1 ground state sum
F=log(Z)=EML-2: the thermodynamic potential is always one log above Z
Onsager=EML-3: only known EXACT solution in 2D stat mech lives at EML-3
